lede: A look at April 14 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including John Shea and Philip III of Spain, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: 2024 Persian Gulf floods
events.desc: Flooding in the Persian Gulf starts, killing 19 in Oman.

events.title: Oregon Supreme Court
events.desc: The Oregon Supreme Court nullifies marriage licenses issued to same-sex couples a year earlier by Multnomah County.

events.title: Venezuela
events.desc: Venezuelan president Hugo Chávez returns to office two days after being ousted and arrested by the country's military.

events.title: 1999 Sydney hailstorm
events.desc: A severe hailstorm strikes Sydney, Australia causing A$2.3 billion in insured damages, the most costly natural disaster in Australian history.

events.title: Georgia (country)
events.desc: The Republic of Georgia introduces the post of President following its declaration of independence from the Soviet Union.

events.title: 1978 Georgian demonstrations
events.desc: Tbilisi demonstrations: Thousands of Georgians demonstrate against Soviet attempts to change the constitutional status of the Georgian language.

events.title: Gnassingbé Eyadéma
events.desc: Gnassingbé Eyadéma overthrows Nicolas Grunitzky and installs himself as the new President of Togo, a title he will hold for the next 38 years.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Royal Marines land in Namsos, Norway, preceding a larger force which will arrive two days later.

events.title: Hauser Dam
events.desc: Hauser Dam, a steel dam on the Missouri River in Montana, fails, sending a surge of water 25 to 30 feet (7.6 to 9.1 m) high downstream.

events.title: Azusa Street Revival
events.desc: The first meeting of the Azusa Street Revival, which will launch Pentecostalism as a worldwide movement, is held in Los Angeles.

births.name: Philip III of Spain
births.line: King of Spain and Portugal from 1598 to 1621

births.name: Peter Behrens
births.line: German architect, designed the AEG turbine factory

deaths.text: Bernie Madoff, American mastermind of the world's largest Ponzi scheme
Simone de Beauvoir, French novelist and philosopher
L. L. Zamenhof, Polish physician and linguist, created Esperanto
George Frideric Handel, German-English organist and composer

observances.text: Christian feast day: Anthony, John, and Eustathius
Christian feast day: Henry Beard Delany (U.S. Episcopal Church)
